test_cases.description: FRAME_SIZES is a frozen array (Object.isFrozen() === true)
FRAME_SIZES contains exactly [1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]
FRAME_SIZES elements are in strictly ascending order
assertions: Each element is strictly greater than the previous

test_cases.description: All FRAME_SIZES elements are powers of 2
Attempting to mutate FRAME_SIZES does not change it (frozen)
assertions: push/assignment/pop have no effect, array stays [1024, 4096, 16384, 65536]

test_cases.description: FRAME_SIZES is the same reference across multiple accesses
padMessage with empty Uint8Array returns frame of size 1024 and round-trips
padMessage with a small message (10 bytes) returns 1024-byte frame with message at offset 0
test_cases.input_description: Uint8Array of 10 bytes with known content
assertions: last 2 bytes encode 10 as big-endian uint16

test_cases.description: Messages up to 1022 bytes select the 1024-byte frame (1022 + 2 = 1024)
Message of 1023 bytes exceeds 1024 frame capacity, selects 4096-byte frame
Message of 4094 bytes exactly fills 4096-byte frame
Message of 4095 bytes exceeds 4096 frame capacity, selects 16384-byte frame
Message of 16382 bytes exactly fills 16384-byte frame
Message of 16383 bytes exceeds 16384 frame capacity, selects 65536-byte frame
Message of 65534 bytes exactly fills 65536-byte frame (max allowed)
Last 2 bytes of padded frame encode the original message length as big-endian uint16
padMessage returns a new Uint8Array that does not alias the input buffer
padMessage output length is always one of the FRAME_SIZES values
Padding two identical messages produces different padding bytes (probabilistic)
assertions: Padding regions differ between two calls (with very high probability)

test_cases.description: Messages containing 0x00 bytes round-trip correctly
test_cases.input_description: Uint8Array with embedded zero bytes like [0, 1, 0, 2, 0]

test_cases.description: Round-trip property: unpadMessage(padMessage(m)) equals m for many random inputs
assertions: unpadMessage(padMessage(m)) deep equals m for 50 random messages of varying sizes

test_cases.description: padMessage throws RangeError when messageBytes.length > 65534
padMessage throws RangeError for message length 65536 (above max)
unpadMessage correctly extracts message from a valid padded frame
assertions: Returned Uint8Array matches the original message

test_cases.description: unpadMessage returns a new Uint8Array that does not alias the input buffer
unpadMessage throws RangeError when paddedBytes is empty (length 0)
unpadMessage throws RangeError when paddedBytes has only 1 byte
unpadMessage throws RangeError when length suffix claims more bytes than available
test_cases.input_description: 4-byte frame with last 2 bytes encoding length 5 (exceeds 4 - 2 = 2 available bytes)

test_cases.description: Modifying the length suffix of a valid padded frame causes unpadMessage to return wrong data or throw
test_cases.input_description: Valid padded frame with last 2 bytes modified to claim length > actual message
assertions: Either throws RangeError or returned bytes differ from original message

test_cases.description: unpadMessage works correctly on a 2-byte frame encoding length 0 (empty message)
unpadMessage works when decoded length equals paddedBytes.length - 2 (no padding gap)
test_cases.input_description: Frame where message fills all space except the 2-byte suffix
assertions: Returns Uint8Array with correct message bytes
